failure_signature: bought old leaders in a post-panic rebound
lesson: After a panic, the rebound is led by beaten-down losers, not the old leaders; wait for a new base, a new follow-through, and a new leadership list before buying strength.

failure_signature: chased laggard catch-up as a fresh entry
lesson: Laggards launching en masse is the theme's public-laggard timer, not a buy signal; in the public phase it is the momentum-crash catch-up trap.

failure_signature: bought a high-volume stall expecting one more leg after consensus
lesson: At the weekly scale the 'divergence-to-consensus for one more leg' read is a day-trade artifact; here it coincides with a distribution top — high-volume stalling is the exit.

failure_signature: stayed in a theme after its leader broke down on volume
lesson: A leader breaking key moving averages on volume is the number-one signal of a theme cycle turning; re-read the theme clock before defending the stock.
One link's earnings forecast the next link's orders; a customer's capex guide is the supplier's revenue lead — the cleanest positive transfer, run on a quarterly timescale.

failure_signature: sized a heat spike with no industry confluence as a real theme
lesson: A theme's size is its confluence count — industry fact, institutional consensus, public participation, policy tailwind, cross-market confirmation; heat with zero confluence is a chip game.
The edge is a pre-registered fundamental surprise from industry insight, not anomaly arbitrage; register what the world will show first, before it does.
In a correction, cash is a position; no new entries, only reduce.
The thesis card is the plan; trade the plan, not the tape.

failure_signature: held on willpower after the thesis broke
lesson: Conviction to hold comes from the thesis-price matrix, not willpower; a good price on a broken thesis is an exit, not a reason to stay.
The new cycle's leaders are usually not the last cycle's; after a panic, watch for the new leadership list rather than the old names.

failure_signature: thesis core assumption had no falsifiable verification node
named_analog: Peloton 2021 — demand pull-forward vs behavior shift
lesson: Distinguishable assumptions (demand pull-forward vs permanent behavior shift) must be written as explicit verification nodes, or two opposite theses look identical in a bull market — the counter-thesis was the truth.

Template for every invested theme, four prose answers: (1) the thesis — name customers, competitors, bottlenecks, no market language; (2) why now — the inflection; (3) the smart short case; (4) what the world shows first — a pre-registered surprise with calendar-anchored verification nodes.

The old single-scale daily sentiment cycle is not discarded but fractalized into three nested clocks — market state (read daily), theme lifecycle (weekly), stock stage (weekly); a cycle word is meaningless until you name which scale it belongs to.

The felt money-making vs money-losing effect migrates to computable breadth — % above the 200-day line, net new 52-week highs, advance/decline — feeding the three market states; measure it, do not eyeball it.

failure_signature: ranked a strong chart with no industry-profit case as the leader
lesson: A leader is industry-profit bearer x market confirmation, both required; candidates come only from the thesis card's profit-attribution chain, never from price strength alone — confirmation without profit is a chip game, profit without confirmation a value trap.

failure_signature: held the theme after its leader broke down and dragged the whole group
lesson: The A-share limit-down leader that drags its sector has no US structural analog; it migrates to a leader breaking a key moving average on volume — the number-one signal the whole theme is turning, forcing a theme-clock re-read before defending any name in the group.

failure_signature: chased accelerating rotation as fresh opportunity instead of the tail
lesson: Accelerating rotation — fringe names dancing, delivery no longer moving price — is the theme's exhaustion criterion, the tail end of the cycle, not a fresh opportunity to chase.

failure_signature: migrated the intraday weak-turns-strong read to the weekly horizon
lesson: 'Refusing to weaken is strength' splits by scale: the intraday weak-turns-strong read is a tombstone — a T+1 day-trade artifact, do not migrate; at the weekly scale it survives transformed — a fast reclaim after a shakeout, undercut-and-rally, or an earnings gap-up that does not fill are institutions supporting — but it only confirms an existing thesis candidate, never discovers a new one.

The A-share reflex 'when insiders cut, you run' migrates to US insider-and-dilution alerts — Form 4 sales, ATM offerings, S-3 shelf registrations; the discipline formalizes into a rule once the data feed lands (deferred to P5 feeds).

failure_signature: read a candlestick topping pattern as the signal instead of the stage
lesson: Candlestick topping patterns (evening star and kin) migrate from chart-shape reading to stock-stage top language: a climax run far above the moving averages, or high-volume stalling and distribution — this is trim language, not add language.
